subagent_lanes.scope: Audit A/C/E/F/G/J/L/N/Q/R/W/Z and numeric subway/subway-shuttle route keys, collisions, and mode-qualified key recommendations.
key_findings: Bare subway letters and numeric subway routes lacked stable mode-qualified identity aliases.
No current exact bare-key collisions were found, but mode-qualified keys are safer and deterministic.

subagent_lanes.scope: Audit Q52/Q53, X28/X38, SIM23/SIM24, Bx40/42, M34/M34A, Staten Island express bundles, 15 Express bus routes, and broad route-list records.
key_findings: True route records should remain page-bearing route records.
Aggregate/list context and data-only scope records should likely stay canonical but not produce route pages.
Implemented the suggested pure aggregate route classifier with tests before any materializer page-policy wiring.

subagent_lanes.scope: Audit Q52/Q53, Utica/B46, Webster/Bx41, Bx12, B44, M60, and M34/M34A high _merged_field_values records.
key_findings: Q52/Q53 and Utica/B46 records show high-confidence service-variant contamination: top-level SBS identity with merged limited-stop/LTD values.
Some other hotspots, including B44 SBS, look like healthy evidence accumulation and should not be split solely because aliases or route_id variants accumulated.
A pure classifier guardrail is safer than removing aliases or rewiring page policy in this slice.

subagent_lanes.scope: Reduce noisy advisory route-base candidate edges by making the deterministic parser prefer exact scalar payload.route_id and decline slash-composite route ids.
Audit whether routeRecordScope should drive materializer page policy for aggregate_list_context, data_only_scope, and split_candidate route records.
key_findings: Suppressing split_candidate route pages would delete useful SBS and route-hotspot pages and is not safe as a broad deletion policy.
Suppressing aggregate_list_context pages should wait for writer-content migration/churn review.
Suppressing only data_only_scope removes one count-shaped route page with no canonical route churn.

implemented.summary: Added exported pure parser test hook and changed route candidate base extraction to prefer exact atomic payload route_id. Composite slash route ids now return no advisory base route instead of collapsing to the first member. Tests cover B82E vs B82, S93 vs S53, and Q52/Q53 composite ids.

Added deterministic subway route alias generation for A/C/E/F/G/J/L/N/Q/R/W/Z and numeric 1-7 routes only when route context indicates subway/train mode. Rebuilt materialized aliases; examples include route_a-subway, route_q-subway, route_2-subway, and route_7-subway.

Added pure routeRecordScope classifier for true_route, aggregate_list_context, data_only_scope, and split_candidate records. Tests cover X28/X38 aggregate vs true X28/X38 records, 15 Express bus count scope, M34/M34A split candidate, and the M14 A/D exception.

Extended routeRecordScope to classify records as split_candidate when a single top-level service variant conflicts with merged service_variant, route_type_normalized, or route_type values. Tests cover Q52 SBS and Utica/B46 limited-stop contamination while preserving clean B44 SBS accumulation as true_route.

Added record-aware page path policy and made only data_only_scope route records non-page-bearing. Deterministic materialization removed the count-only route_15-express-bus-battery-pl page while preserving all 321 canonical route records. Aggregate/list context and split-candidate route records remain page-bearing pending writer-content/churn review.

remaining_lanes: Decide whether to wire aggregate route page suppression after a writer-content migration/churn review.
Decide whether to annotate or otherwise handle split-candidate route pages without deleting useful route pages.
Run validate after each further implementation slice and update this or a follow-on review note.
